## Overview

Flexi-Grant (branded Flexigrant) is a cloud-hosted, highly configurable grant-management SaaS platform developed by Fluent Technology Ltd, headquartered in Belfast, Northern Ireland. Founded in 2001, Fluent was acquired by Canadian vertical-software holding company Valsoft Corporation in 2022. Flexi-Grant is widely used across the UK public sector, charitable trusts, community foundations, medical research funders and central government departments. It is available on G-Cloud 14 (Cloud Software / Lot 2).

## Products & Services

- **Flexigrant** — the core SaaS platform: scheme builder, application intake, assessment, award, claims/monitoring, reporting. Non-technical configuration model; suits rapid deployment.
- **Professional services** — implementation, configuration, training delivered as part of the G-Cloud call-off.
- Targeted tiers for 3 to 100+ concurrent users.

## Pricing Model

- Published G-Cloud 14 indicative price: **from ~£37,000 in Year 1** for a 3-user licence configuration, including hosting and support.
- Scales with concurrent users and optional modules. Volume discounts not publicly disclosed.
- Typical public-sector implementation: £50k–£150k Year 1 depending on scope and complexity; ongoing subscription Year 2+ lower once implementation is paid off.

## UK Government Presence

- **G-Cloud 14 listed**: yes — [Flexigrant on Digital Marketplace](https://www.applytosupply.digitalmarketplace.service.gov.uk/g-cloud/services/899316002882517)
- **DOS listed**: not currently featured in DOS6; listing is on G-Cloud.
- **UK data centres**: yes (hosted in UK data centres per vendor documentation).
- **Accreditations**: ISO 9001, ISO 27001, Cyber Essentials Plus.
- Known UK users include NIHR, various UKRI-adjacent funders, charitable trusts, and local authority grants teams.

## Strengths

- Established UK public-sector footprint; low procurement risk via G-Cloud 14.
- Non-technical configuration reduces dependence on vendor for routine changes.
- ISO 27001 + Cyber Essentials Plus — aligns with Secure by Design expectations.
- Vendor pricing transparency better than most G-Cloud peers (clear starting point).
- Proven rapid deployment (weeks to months for standard implementations).

## Weaknesses

- Not Green Book / TAG / BCR aware — requires customisation for transport appraisal.
- Multi-authority federation (DfT + 9 MCAs) not its native pattern; typically single-grantor deployments.
- User-based pricing may escalate at scale across DfT + 9 MCAs + external assessors.
- Valsoft ownership — vertical-software roll-up investor model; watch for product-investment signals.
- Not GOV.UK Design System native — applicant UX may require theming effort to match One Login patterns.

## Projects Referenced In

- DfT Transforming City Regions Funding (discovery, 2026-04-20)
